1	/**
2	 * Clerk public types.
3	 *
4	 * All types exported from @shardworks/clerk-apparatus.
5	 */
6	
7	import type {
8	  WritTypeConfig,
9	  WritTypeStateAttr,
10	  WritTypeStateClassification,
11	} from './writ-type-config.ts';
12	
13	// ── Writ phase ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
14	
15	/**
16	 * The mandate writ type's lifecycle states.
17	 *
18	 * This union is mandate-specific — it enumerates the six states the built-in
19	 * mandate `WritTypeConfig` declares and is preserved for callers that
20	 * knowingly work exclusively with mandate writs. The structural type of
21	 * `WritDoc.phase` is `string` so any plugin-registered writ type's state
22	 * name can round-trip through the book; callers that need a mandate-phase
23	 * typed value should downcast explicitly.
24	 *
25	 * Mandate transitions:
26	 *   new    → open       (publish)   — draft enters the queue
27	 *   new    → cancelled  (cancel)
28	 *   open   → completed  (complete)
29	 *   open   → failed     (fail)
30	 *   open   → cancelled  (cancel)
31	 *   open   → stuck      (engine failure cascade)
32	 *   stuck  → open       (recovery/retry resumes execution)
33	 *   stuck  → failed     (obligation abandoned)
34	 *   stuck  → cancelled  (obligation withdrawn)
35	 *
36	 * completed, failed, cancelled are terminal — no further transitions.
37	 * stuck is non-terminal — it represents a "needs attention" state.
38	 */
39	export type WritPhase = 'new' | 'open' | 'stuck' | 'completed' | 'failed' | 'cancelled';
40	
41	// ── Documents ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
42	
43	// ── status['clerk'] sub-slot ─────────────────────────────────────────
44	
45	/**
46	 * The Clerk-owned sub-slot of `WritDoc.status`.
47	 *
48	 * Written by the children-behavior cascade engine at the moment a parent's
49	 * terminal transition fires: the engine records the *immediate* triggering
50	 * child's id so downstream observers (notably the Reckoner) can chase the
51	 * cascade chain back to the leaf cause without parsing the parent's
52	 * resolution string.
53	 *
54	 * Ownership: Clerk writes; downstream consumers read. Plugin convention is
55	 * the same as for any `status[<pluginId>]` sub-slot — see
56	 * `setWritStatus()` and the spec/status convention notes on `ClerkApi`.
57	 *
58	 * The slot is forward-only: there is no migration or backfill for writs
59	 * that already terminal'd before this slot existed. Pre-existing terminal
60	 * writs do not re-emit pulses, so the absence of a slot on a historical
61	 * writ is harmless.
62	 */
63	export interface ClerkWritStatus {
64	  /**
65	   * Id of the immediate child whose terminal transition fired the
66	   * children-behavior cascade onto this writ. Absent on writs that
67	   * reached terminal through a direct transition (no cascade).
68	   *
69	   * For multi-level cascades (root → mid → leaf), each level's parent
70	   * carries its own immediate triggering child id; consumers walk the
71	   * chain by reading each successive writ's `status['clerk']
72	   * .triggeringChildId`.
73	   */
74	  triggeringChildId?: string;
75	}
76	
77	/**
78	 * Plugin id stamped on the Clerk's own observation sub-slot
79	 * (`status['clerk']`). Held as a constant so the engine and the
80	 * `setWritStatus` consumer agree on the key.
81	 */
82	export const CLERK_PLUGIN_ID = 'clerk';
83	
84	/**
85	 * A writ document as stored in The Stacks.
86	 */
87	export interface WritDoc {
88	  /** Index signature required to satisfy BookEntry constraint. */
89	  [key: string]: unknown;
90	  /** Unique writ id (`w-{base36_timestamp}-{hex_random}`). Sortable by creation time. */
91	  id: string;
92	  /** Writ type — must be a type declared in guild config, or a built-in type. */
93	  type: string;
94	  /**
95	   * Current lifecycle state name (Clerk-owned; spec side of the spec/status
96	   * split). Structurally typed as `string` so any plugin-registered writ
97	   * type's state name can round-trip through the book. For mandate-typed
98	   * writs the value is constrained to a `WritPhase` literal at runtime, but
99	   * callers that need a narrower static type should downcast explicitly.
100	   */
101	  phase: string;
102	  /**
103	   * Plugin-owned observation slot (status side of the spec/status split).
104	   *
105	   * The observation slot is a plugin-keyed map (`Record<PluginId,
106	   * unknown>`): each top-level key is a plugin id, and the value is an
107	   * arbitrary shape that plugin publishes for post-hoc observation.
108	   * Ownership is convention-only — plugin `X` writes only to `status[X]`.
109	   *
110	   * There is exactly one sanctioned slot-write path:
111	   * `ClerkApi.setWritStatus(writId, pluginId, value)`, which performs a
112	   * transactional read-modify-write on the sub-slot keyed by `pluginId`
113	   * so sibling sub-slots are preserved under concurrent writers.
114	   * `transition()` silently strips `status` from its body, and the
115	   * generic `put()` / `patch()` paths on the writs book are not
116	   * supported slot-write mechanisms — every route other than
117	   * `setWritStatus()` would wholesale-replace the slot and clobber
118	   * sibling sub-slots. Readers access `writ.status?.[pluginId]` directly.
119	   *
120	   * The slot survives terminal phase transitions.
121	   */
122	  status?: Record<string, unknown>;
123	  /**
124	   * Plugin-owned metadata slot — sibling to `status` but reserved for
125	   * metadata-shape data (provenance, cross-references, classification
126	   * tags, configuration extensions) attached at writ creation or
127	   * registration time, rather than the post-hoc observation that
128	   * `status` records.
129	   *
130	   * The semantic distinction matters: `status` is for what a plugin
131	   * has *observed* about a writ after the fact (a stuck cause, a
132	   * triggering child, a gate result); `ext` is for what a plugin needs
133	   * the writ to *carry* as an attribute of its identity (a petition id,
134	   * a classifier tag, a foreign-system reference). Picking the wrong
135	   * slot layers metadata under an observation contract or vice versa,
136	   * so plugin authors should choose consciously.
137	   *
138	   * Structurally identical to `status`: a plugin-keyed map
139	   * (`Record<PluginId, unknown>`) where each top-level key is a plugin
140	   * id and the value is an arbitrary opaque shape. Ownership is
141	   * convention-only — plugin `X` writes only to `ext[X]`.
142	   *
143	   * There is exactly one sanctioned slot-write path:
144	   * `ClerkApi.setWritExt(writId, pluginId, value)`, which performs a
145	   * transactional read-modify-write on the sub-slot keyed by `pluginId`
146	   * so sibling sub-slots are preserved under concurrent writers.
147	   * `transition()` silently strips `ext` from its body, and the
148	   * generic `put()` / `patch()` paths on the writs book are not
149	   * supported slot-write mechanisms — every route other than
150	   * `setWritExt()` would wholesale-replace the slot and clobber
151	   * sibling sub-slots. Readers access `writ.ext?.[pluginId]` directly.
152	   *
153	   * Optional and absent by default — a freshly-posted writ reads
154	   * `ext === undefined` until a plugin writes its first sub-slot. The
155	   * slot survives terminal phase transitions.
156	   */
157	  ext?: Record<string, unknown>;
158	  /** Short human-readable title. */
159	  title: string;
160	  /** Detail text. */
161	  body: string;
162	  /** Target codex name. */
163	  codex?: string;
164	  /** Parent writ id. Absent on root writs. Immutable after creation. */
165	  parentId?: string;
166	  /** ISO timestamp when the writ was created. */
167	  createdAt: string;
168	  /** ISO timestamp of the last mutation. */
169	  updatedAt: string;
170	  /** ISO timestamp when the writ reached a terminal state. */
171	  resolvedAt?: string;
172	  /** Summary of how the writ resolved (set on any terminal transition). */
173	  resolution?: string;
174	}
175	
176	// ── Requests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
177	
178	/**
179	 * Request to post a new commission (create a writ).
180	 */
181	/**
182	 * Request to edit a writ. Title and body can be edited in any status.
183	 * Type and codex can only be changed while the writ is in 'new' status.
184	 * All fields are optional — only provided fields are updated.
185	 */
186	export interface EditWritRequest {
187	  /** Writ id. */
188	  id: string;
189	  /** New title. */
190	  title?: string;
191	  /** New body text. */
192	  body?: string;
193	  /** New writ type. Must be a valid declared type. */
194	  type?: string;
195	  /** New target codex name. Pass empty string to clear. */
196	  codex?: string;
197	}
198	
199	export interface PostCommissionRequest {
200	  /**
201	   * Writ type. Defaults to the guild's configured defaultType, or "mandate"
202	   * if no default is configured. Must be a registered writ type.
203	   */
204	  type?: string;
205	  /** Short human-readable title describing the work. */
206	  title: string;
207	  /** Detail text. */
208	  body: string;
209	  /** Optional target codex name. */
210	  codex?: string;
211	  /**
212	   * Create this writ as a child of the specified parent writ.
213	   * The parent must not be in a terminal state.
214	   */
215	  parentId?: string;
216	}
217	
218	// ── Filters ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
219	
220	/**
221	 * Filters for listing writs.
222	 */
223	export interface WritFilters {
224	  /**
225	   * Filter by phase. Accepts a single phase or an array of phases (OR).
226	   *
227	   * `phase` is mandate-scoped at the WHERE-clause level: when `phase` is
228	   * supplied without `type`, the implementation implicitly adds
229	   * `type = 'mandate'` so a non-mandate writ that happens to declare an
230	   * `open` state cannot match a `phase: 'open'` filter unscoped to its
231	   * type. To query a non-mandate type's same-named state, pass both
232	   * `type` and `phase` together (D7).
233	   */
234	  phase?: WritPhase | WritPhase[];
235	  /** Filter by writ type. Accepts a single type or an array of types (OR). */
236	  type?: string | string[];
237	  /**
238	   * Filter by state classification. Accepts a single classification or an
239	   * array (OR). Type-agnostic: applies across every registered writ type.
240	   * The closed three-value enum mirrors `WritTypeStateClassification`.
241	   */
242	  classification?:
243	    | WritTypeStateClassification
244	    | WritTypeStateClassification[];
245	  /** Filter to children of this parent writ. */
246	  parentId?: string;
247	  /** Maximum number of results (default: 20). */
248	  limit?: number;
249	  /** Number of results to skip. */
250	  offset?: number;
251	}
252	
253	// ── Presentation projections ─────────────────────────────────────────
254	
255	/**
256	 * Classification value as embedded on writ-bearing tool responses.
257	 *
258	 * Domain-side classifier helpers (`ClerkApi.isInitial`/`isActive`/
259	 * `isTerminal`) throw on an unregistered type or undeclared state — the
260	 * classifier preserves its fail-loud contract. The presentation projection
261	 * is tolerant and surfaces `'unknown'` for the same conditions so a single
262	 * legacy row cannot crash a list/tree/show response.
263	 */
264	export type WritPresentationClassification = 'initial' | 'active' | 'terminal' | 'unknown';
265	
266	/**
267	 * Per-row presentation projection. `writ-list` returns rows of this
268	 * shape; `writ-tree`'s `WritTree.writ` and `writ-show`'s top-level writ
269	 * extend `WritDoc` with the same two derived fields.
270	 */
271	export interface WritWithPresentation extends WritDoc {
272	  /**
273	   * Classification of the writ's current state in its type config.
274	   * `'unknown'` indicates the writ's type is unregistered or its phase
275	   * is not declared in the registered config (presentation tolerance).
276	   */
277	  classification: WritPresentationClassification;
278	  /**
279	   * Outbound transition names declared on the writ's current state in
280	   * its type config. Empty for terminal states and for unknown
281	   * classifications.
282	   */
283	  allowedTransitions: string[];
284	}
285	
286	/**
287	 * Compact parent/child reference shape used on `writ-show`'s `parent`
288	 * field and `children.items` entries. Carries the same `classification`
289	 * and `allowedTransitions` derivations as a full row so a renderer can
290	 * pick badge classes and action affordances uniformly.
291	 */
292	export interface WritReferenceWithPresentation {
293	  /** Writ id. */
294	  id: string;
295	  /** Writ title. */
296	  title: string;
297	  /** Writ type. */
298	  type: string;
299	  /** Current lifecycle state name. */
300	  phase: string;
301	  /** Classification of the writ's current state in its type config. */
302	  classification: WritPresentationClassification;
303	  /** Outbound transitions from the writ's current state. */
304	  allowedTransitions: string[];
305	}
306	
307	// ── Tree shapes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
308	
309	/**
310	 * A nested writ-and-children tree node, returned by `ClerkApi.tree()`.
311	 *
312	 * The shape is the writ document plus a recursively-typed `children` array
313	 * of further `WritTree` nodes. Mirrors ratchet's `ClickTree` so the same
314	 * mental model and rendering idioms apply across apparatus.
315	 *
316	 * Each node's `writ` carries the standard `WritDoc` fields plus the
317	 * presentation projection (`classification`, `allowedTransitions`) so
318	 * tree renderers can pick glyphs and action affordances per node without
319	 * reaching back into the type-config registry.
320	 */
321	export interface WritTree {
322	  /** The writ document at this node, with embedded presentation fields. */
323	  writ: WritWithPresentation;
324	  /** Direct children, each shaped as a `WritTree`. */
325	  children: WritTree[];
326	}
327	
328	/**
329	 * Parameters for `ClerkApi.tree()`.
330	 *
331	 * - `rootId` switches between forest mode (omit) and subtree mode
332	 *   (single root). In subtree mode the returned array always has at most
333	 *   one element.
334	 * - `phase` and `type` apply prune semantics: any node failing the filter
335	 *   is dropped along with its entire subtree.
336	 * - `depth` caps recursion. The node at depth = `depth` is included but its
337	 *   children are not. `depth: 0` returns roots only.
338	 * - `rootLimit` and `rootOffset` page across the *root* layer of forest
339	 *   mode (preserves the page's `Load more` UX). They are ignored when
340	 *   `rootId` is supplied.
341	 *
342	 * In forest mode roots are returned in `createdAt desc` order (newest
343	 * first) to match `list()` and the existing writs-page UX. Children
344	 * within each subtree stay in `createdAt asc` order (oldest first) so the
345	 * visual shape of the tree is stable across sort/filter changes.
346	 */
347	export interface WritTreeParams {
348	  /** Restrict to the subtree rooted at this writ id. */
349	  rootId?: string;
350	  /**
351	   * Filter by writ phase (single or OR-list); prunes non-matching
352	   * subtrees. Mandate-scoped at the same level as `WritFilters.phase` —
353	   * see that field's docstring (D7).
354	   */
355	  phase?: WritPhase | WritPhase[];
356	  /** Filter by writ type (single or OR-list); prunes non-matching subtrees. */
357	  type?: string | string[];
358	  /**
359	   * Filter by state classification (single or OR-list); prunes
360	   * non-matching subtrees. Type-agnostic — applies across every
361	   * registered writ type.
362	   */
363	  classification?:
364	    | WritTypeStateClassification
365	    | WritTypeStateClassification[];
366	  /** Maximum recursion depth (0 = roots only). Node at the cap is included. */
367	  depth?: number;
368	  /** Root-slice limit for forest mode (default: unbounded). */
369	  rootLimit?: number;
370	  /** Root-slice offset for forest mode (default: 0). */
371	  rootOffset?: number;
372	}
373	
374	// ── Configuration ───────────────────────────────────────────────
375	
376	/**
377	 * Clerk apparatus configuration — lives under the `clerk` key in guild.json.
378	 *
379	 * Writ types are now contributed exclusively via
380	 * `ClerkApi.registerWritType` from a plugin's own `start()`. There is no
381	 * guild-config `writTypes` field; the operator's only handle on the writ-
382	 * type registry from guild config is the `defaultType` selection (which is
383	 * validated against the registry once startup seals).
384	 */
385	export interface ClerkConfig {
386	  /** Default writ type when commission-post is called without a type (default: "mandate"). Validated against the writ-type registry at startup; an unregistered name fails fast. */
387	  defaultType?: string;
388	}
389	
390	// Augment GuildConfig so `guild().guildConfig().clerk` is typed without
391	// requiring a manual type parameter at the call site.
392	declare module '@shardworks/nexus-core' {
393	  interface GuildConfig {
394	    clerk?: ClerkConfig;
395	  }
396	}
397	
398	// ── Link documents ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
399	
400	/**
401	 * A link document as stored in The Stacks (clerk/links book).
402	 *
403	 * A writ link has two complementary identifiers for its relationship:
404	 *
405	 *   - `label` is a casual, human-facing string. It is open-ended, normalized
406	 *     at write time via the link-label normalization pipeline (lowercase,
407	 *     trim, camelCase split, snake_case/kebab-case split, whitespace
408	 *     collapse). Variant spellings of the same label collapse to a single
409	 *     canonical form; distinct labels remain distinct. Normalization is
410	 *     purely syntactic — it is NOT synonymy. `requires` and `depends on`
411	 *     normalize to different strings; callers that want to treat them as the
412	 *     same relationship should attach the same `kind` to both.
413	 *
414	 *   - `kind` is the load-bearing identifier. It is a stable, plugin-owned id
415	 *     drawn from the kit-contributed link-kind registry, and identifies the
416	 *     specific relationship a downstream consumer wants to react to
417	 *     (rendering parent/child, gating publish on `refines`, etc.). `null`
418	 *     when the caller treated this link as a casual label.
419	 */
420	export interface WritLinkDoc {
421	  /** Index signature required to satisfy BookEntry constraint. */
422	  [key: string]: unknown;
423	  /** Deterministic composite key: `{sourceId}:{targetId}:{normalized label}`. */
424	  id: string;
425	  /** The writ that is the origin of this relationship. */
426	  sourceId: string;
427	  /** The writ that is the target of this relationship. */
428	  targetId: string;
429	  /**
430	   * Casual relationship label — an open string normalized syntactically at
431	   * write time (e.g. "fixes", "retries", "supersedes", "duplicates",
432	   * "depends on"). NOT a synonymy layer. For load-bearing identity, use
433	   * `kind`.
434	   */
435	  label: string;
436	  /**
437	   * Load-bearing identifier for this relationship. When non-null, references
438	   * a registered kind id from the `linkKinds` kit registry and is the field
439	   * downstream consumers key on. Always present on each row (`null` when
440	   * the caller did not attach a kind).
441	   */
442	  kind: string | null;
443	  /** ISO timestamp when the link was created. */
444	  createdAt: string;
445	}
446	
447	// ── Link kind registry ───────────────────────────────────────────────
448	
449	/**
450	 * A link-kind contribution declared by a kit's `linkKinds` array.
451	 *
452	 * Kit authors use this shape when populating `ClerkKit.linkKinds`. Kind ids
453	 * must be prefixed with the contributing plugin's id followed by `.` and a
454	 * kebab-case suffix — e.g. kit `astrolabe` contributing `astrolabe.refines`.
455	 * The registry-projection view (returned by `listKinds()`) is `LinkKindDoc`,
456	 * which embeds the resolved owner plugin id; kit authors do not repeat their
457	 * plugin id on each entry.
458	 */
459	export interface KindEntry {
460	  /**
461	   * Fully-qualified kind id. Must begin with `{pluginId}.` and be followed
462	   * by a non-empty kebab-case suffix (lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens,
463	   * not starting or ending with a hyphen).
464	   *
465	   * Carve-out: contributions from the Clerk plugin itself (where the
466	   * contributing plugin id equals the Clerk's own id) may additionally use
467	   * a bare kebab id without a `{pluginId}.` prefix — e.g. `depends-on`.
468	   * The Clerk owns the writ link substrate and therefore owns the
469	   * unprefixed namespace within that substrate. The carve-out is exclusive
470	   * to Clerk; every other plugin must continue to use the prefixed form.
471	   */
472	  id: string;
473	  /** Human-readable description of the relationship this kind denotes. */
474	  description: string;
475	}
476	
477	/**
478	 * A link-kind record as projected by `listKinds()`.
479	 *
480	 * Unlike `KindEntry`, this shape embeds the resolved owner plugin id so
481	 * consumers of the registry do not need to parse it back out of `id`.
482	 */
483	export interface LinkKindDoc {
484	  /** Fully-qualified kind id. */
485	  id: string;
486	  /** Plugin id of the kit that contributed this kind. */
487	  ownerPlugin: string;
488	  /** Human-readable description of this kind. */
489	  description: string;
490	}
491	
492	/**
493	 * Result of querying links for a writ — both directions in one response.
494	 */
495	export interface WritLinks {
496	  /** Links where this writ is the source (this writ → other writ). */
497	  outbound: WritLinkDoc[];
498	  /** Links where this writ is the target (other writ → this writ). */
499	  inbound: WritLinkDoc[];
500	}
501	
502	// ── Writ type metadata ──────────────────────────────────────────────
503	
504	/**
505	 * Per-state metadata projected from a `WritTypeStateDefinition` for the
506	 * `/api/writ/types` and `nsg writ types` surfaces. Carries everything a
507	 * presentation layer needs to derive vocabulary, badges, and action
508	 * affordances without consulting the type-config registry per row.
509	 */
510	export interface WritTypeStateInfo {
511	  /** State name. */
512	  name: string;
513	  /** State's role in the lifecycle. */
514	  classification: WritTypeStateClassification;
515	  /** Per-state attribute tags (e.g. `success`, `failure`, `cancelled`). */
516	  attrs: WritTypeStateAttr[];
517	  /** Outbound transitions declared on this state. */
518	  allowedTransitions: string[];
519	}
520	
521	/**
522	 * Metadata for a registered writ type, returned by listWritTypes().
523	 */
524	export interface WritTypeInfo {
525	  /** The writ type name. */
526	  name: string;
527	  /**
528	   * Reserved for a future config-carried description field. Always `null`
529	   * today — `WritTypeConfig` does not currently model a description, and
530	   * the legacy guild-config / kit-channel description fields are gone.
531	   */
532	  description: string | null;
533	  /**
534	   * Origin of this type. `"builtin"` is reserved for `mandate`, which the
535	   * Clerk plugin registers from its own `start()`; every other registered
536	   * type carries `"plugin"`. The Clerk does not track the calling plugin's
537	   * id — there is no implicit hand-off of caller identity into
538	   * `registerWritType`.
539	   */
540	  source: 'builtin' | 'plugin';
541	  /** Whether this is the guild's default writ type. */
542	  isDefault: boolean;
543	  /**
544	   * The full state catalogue for this type — one entry per state declared
545	   * in the type's registered `WritTypeConfig`, in the order it was
546	   * declared. Each entry projects the state's name, classification, attrs,
547	   * and outbound transitions so a presentation layer can derive vocabulary
548	   * (badges, indicators, action labels) without a separate registry
549	   * lookup.
550	   */
551	  states: WritTypeStateInfo[];
552	}
553	
554	// ── API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
555	
556	/**
557	 * The Clerk's runtime API — retrieved via guild().apparatus<ClerkApi>('clerk').
558	 */
559	export interface ClerkApi {
560	  /**
561	   * Post a new commission, creating a writ in its registered type's
562	   * declared `initial` state. The writ type must be registered with
563	   * `registerWritType`; an unknown type is rejected. The caller decides
564	   * whether to advance the writ further (e.g. `commission-post` auto-
565	   * publishes mandate writs to `open` by default — see that tool); the
566	   * `post()` API itself is type-agnostic and never auto-advances.
567	   */
568	  post(request: PostCommissionRequest): Promise<WritDoc>;
569	
570	  /**
571	   * Show a writ by id. Throws if not found.
572	   */
573	  show(id: string): Promise<WritDoc>;
574	
575	  /**
576	   * Resolve a writ id prefix to the full id. Mirrors ratchet's resolveId
577	   * for clicks — callers may pass the short display form (e.g. `w-mo2xi6pt`)
578	   * and receive the full id back. Throws when no writ matches or when the
579	   * prefix is ambiguous.
580	   */
581	  resolveId(prefix: string): Promise<string>;
582	
583	  /**
584	   * List writs with optional filters, ordered by createdAt descending.
585	   */
586	  list(filters?: WritFilters): Promise<WritDoc[]>;
587	
588	  /**
589	   * Count writs matching optional filters.
590	   */
591	  count(filters?: WritFilters): Promise<number>;
592	
593	  /**
594	   * Count writs currently in any `active`-classified state across every
595	   * registered writ type.
596	   *
597	   * Walks the writ-type registry on each call (no caching) and composes a
598	   * per-type OR-form query of the shape `[type, phase IN [active-states-of-type]]`.
599	   * Returns `0` when the registry is empty, when no registered type
600	   * declares any `active` state, or when no rows match.
601	   *
602	   * This is the classification-driven primitive behind the Reckoner's
603	   * drain detector and any future apparatus that needs a multi-type
604	   * "is anything in flight?" count. Existing per-phase counts (e.g.
605	   * `count({ phase: 'open' })`) remain available for type-specific reads.
606	   */
607	  countActive(): Promise<number>;
608	
609	  /**
610	   * Walk the writ hierarchy and return a forest (or a single subtree when
611	   * `rootId` is provided). Mirrors ratchet's `tree()` precedent.
612	   *
613	   * Filters apply with prune semantics: a node failing `phase` or `type`
614	   * is excluded together with its entire subtree. The `depth` cap halts
615	   * recursion below — the node at the cap is still included, but no
616	   * descendants of it are walked. `rootLimit` / `rootOffset` page across
617	   * the root layer of forest mode and are ignored when `rootId` is given.
618	   */
619	  tree(params?: WritTreeParams): Promise<WritTree[]>;
620	
621	  /**
622	   * Count all descendants of a writ grouped by phase.
623	   *
624	   * Walks the parent/child tree recursively beneath `writId`, tallying each
625	   * descendant's phase. The root writ (the writ identified by `writId`) is
626	   * excluded from the count — only its descendants contribute. The result is
627	   * a plain object keyed by `WritPhase` with numeric values; phases with no
628	   * matching descendants are simply absent.
629	   *
630	   * This is the traversal primitive behind `writ-show`'s `children.summary`
631	   * field, and is also reusable by any future subtree-oriented tooling (e.g.
632	   * a `writ-tree` apparatus). Throws if the writ does not exist.
633	   */
634	  countDescendantsByPhase(writId: string): Promise<Record<WritPhase, number>>;
635	
636	  /**
637	   * Transition a writ to a new phase, optionally setting additional fields.
638	   * Validates that the transition is legal.
639	   *
640	   * Managed fields supplied in `fields` are silently dropped before the
641	   * patch is applied: `id`, `phase`, `status`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`,
642	   * `resolvedAt`, and `parentId`. `phase` is managed by the state machine;
643	   * the plugin-owned observation slot `status` is writable only via
644	   * `setWritStatus()` (the one sanctioned slot-write path) so that
645	   * sibling sub-slots are preserved under concurrent writers. The generic
646	   * `put()` / `patch()` paths are not supported slot-write mechanisms.
647	   */
648	  transition(id: string, to: WritPhase, fields?: Partial<WritDoc>): Promise<WritDoc>;
649	
650	  /**
651	   * Write a plugin-owned sub-slot of the writ's observation `status` map.
652	   *
653	   * The slot is the "status" side of the spec/status split: plugins publish
654	   * post-hoc observations here (stuck causes, gate state, provenance, …)
655	   * without polluting the Clerk-owned `phase` state machine.
656	   *
657	   * The call is a transactional read-modify-write — the sub-slot keyed by
658	   * `pluginId` is replaced with `value`, but sibling sub-slots owned by
659	   * other plugins are preserved. Writes emit CDC update events like any
660	   * other field change, and survive terminal phase transitions.
661	   *
662	   * Ownership is convention-only: plugin `X` writes only `status[X]`.
663	   * There is no runtime guard.
664	   *
665	   * Returns the updated writ document.
666	   */
667	  setWritStatus(writId: string, pluginId: string, value: unknown): Promise<WritDoc>;
668	
669	  /**
670	   * Write a plugin-owned sub-slot of the writ's metadata `ext` map.
671	   *
672	   * The slot is the metadata sibling to `status`: plugins attach
673	   * metadata-shape data here (petition ids, cross-references,
674	   * classifier tags, configuration extensions) — data that belongs to
675	   * the writ as an attribute of its identity, rather than the post-hoc
676	   * observation `setWritStatus` records.
677	   *
678	   * The call is a transactional read-modify-write — the sub-slot keyed by
679	   * `pluginId` is replaced with `value`, but sibling sub-slots owned by
680	   * other plugins are preserved. Writes emit CDC update events like any
681	   * other field change, and survive terminal phase transitions.
682	   *
683	   * Validation: throws when `writId` is empty, when `pluginId` is empty,
684	   * or when no writ matches `writId`. The `value` is opaque — the Clerk
685	   * does not validate sub-slot contents (mirrors `setWritStatus`).
686	   *
687	   * Each call bumps `updatedAt` to the current ISO timestamp so CDC
688	   * observers keying on the timestamp see the change.
689	   *
690	   * Ownership is convention-only: plugin `X` writes only `ext[X]`.
691	   * There is no runtime guard.
692	   *
693	   * Returns the updated writ document.
694	   */
695	  setWritExt(writId: string, pluginId: string, value: unknown): Promise<WritDoc>;
696	
697	  /**
698	   * Create a typed directional link from one writ to another.
699	   * Both writs must exist. Self-links are rejected. The `label` argument is
700	   * normalized before the composite id is constructed, so variant spellings
701	   * of the same label collapse to a single link. When a link with the same
702	   * canonical composite id already exists, the existing row is returned;
703	   * if `kind` is supplied, it is written onto the existing row (upsert). The
704	   * optional `kind` must reference an id registered in the kit-contributed
705	   * link-kind registry; unknown ids are rejected.
706	   */
707	  link(
708	    sourceId: string,
709	    targetId: string,
710	    label: string,
711	    kind?: string,
712	  ): Promise<WritLinkDoc>;
713	
714	  /**
715	   * Query all links for a writ — both outbound (this writ is the source)
716	   * and inbound (this writ is the target).
717	   */
718	  links(writId: string): Promise<WritLinks>;
719	
720	  /**
721	   * Remove a link. Idempotent — no error if the link does not exist.
722	   */
723	  unlink(sourceId: string, targetId: string, label: string): Promise<void>;
724	
725	  /**
726	   * Edit a writ. Title and body can be edited in any status.
727	   * Type and codex can only be changed while the writ is in 'new' status.
728	   * Only the provided fields are updated. Validates type against
729	   * declared writ types if provided.
730	   */
731	  edit(request: EditWritRequest): Promise<WritDoc>;
732	
733	  /**
734	   * List the writ types registered with the Clerk via `registerWritType`,
735	   * including the Clerk's own built-in `mandate`. Each entry reports the
736	   * name, the (currently-always-null) description, source (`builtin` or
737	   * `plugin`), and whether it is the default type.
738	   */
739	  listWritTypes(): WritTypeInfo[];
740	
741	  /**
742	   * List all registered link kinds contributed by kits.
743	   * Returns the registry-projection view of each kind, including its owner
744	   * plugin id. Returns an empty array when no kinds have been registered.
745	   */
746	  listKinds(): Promise<LinkKindDoc[]>;
747	
748	  /**
749	   * Register a writ type's state machine with the Clerk.
750	   *
751	   * The supplied config is validated via `validateWritTypeConfig` before the
752	   * registry is updated; validator errors propagate verbatim. Registration-
753	   * specific failures (duplicate name, late call after the startup window has
754	   * sealed) are wrapped with a `[clerk] registerWritType:` prefix.
755	   *
756	   * Callable only while the startup window is open — Clerk seals the
757	   * registry on the framework's global `phase:started` signal, after which
758	   * further calls throw. This is the single-surface entry point for plugins
759	   * contributing writ types; there is no parallel kit-channel or guild-config
760	   * registration path.
761	   */
762	  registerWritType(config: WritTypeConfig): void;
763	
764	  /**
765	   * Return the registered `WritTypeConfig` for a writ type.
766	   *
767	   * Returns `undefined` when the name is not registered. Callers that need
768	   * to work with a writ type abstractly (inspect states, render UI labels,
769	   * compose higher-level predicates) should use this accessor instead of
770	   * reaching into the predicate surface with synthetic `WritDoc`s.
771	   */
772	  getWritTypeConfig(name: string): WritTypeConfig | undefined;
773	
774	  /**
775	   * Return `true` when the writ's current state is classified `initial`
776	   * in its type's registered config.
777	   *
778	   * Throws when the writ's stored state is not declared in its type config
779	   * (fail-loud on unknown-state), or when the writ's type is not registered.
780	   */
781	  isInitial(writ: WritDoc): boolean;
782	
783	  /**
784	   * Return `true` when the writ's current state is classified `active`
785	   * in its type's registered config.
786	   *
787	   * Throws when the writ's stored state is not declared in its type config
788	   * (fail-loud on unknown-state), or when the writ's type is not registered.
789	   */
790	  isActive(writ: WritDoc): boolean;
791	
792	  /**
793	   * Return `true` when the writ's current state is classified `terminal`
794	   * in its type's registered config.
795	   *
796	   * Throws when the writ's stored state is not declared in its type config
797	   * (fail-loud on unknown-state), or when the writ's type is not registered.
798	   */
799	  isTerminal(writ: WritDoc): boolean;
800	}

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * The Spider — public types.
3	 *
4	 * Rig and engine data model, CrawlResult, SpiderApi, and configuration.
5	 * Engine yield shapes (DraftYields, SealYields) live here too so downstream
6	 * packages can import them without depending on the engine implementation files.
7	 */
8	
9	import type { ZodSchema } from 'zod';
10	
11	// ── Engine instance status ────────────────────────────────────────────
12	
13	/**
14	 * Engine status — the full lifecycle of a single engine slot within a rig.
15	 *
16	 * 'pending'   — awaiting dispatch. Covers both "not yet tried" and "held"
17	 *               engines; a hold is represented as `pending` + `holdReason`
18	 *               / `holdUntil` metadata on the engine instance. The
19	 *               dispatch predicate is the sole arbiter of when a pending
20	 *               engine actually runs.
21	 * 'running'   — currently executing (either a clockwork run in progress
22	 *               or a launched anima session being polled by tryCollect).
23	 * 'completed' — finished successfully. `attempts[-1]` carries the yields.
24	 * 'failed'    — terminally failed (retry budget exhausted or the failure
25	 *               was definitional).
26	 * 'cancelled' — cancelled by operator action or by cascade from a failed
27	 *               upstream engine.
28	 * 'skipped'   — `when` condition evaluated false; the engine was never
29	 *               run and its downstream has cascade-skipped any conditionals.
30	 *
31	 * There is no `'blocked'` value: holds are `'pending'` with hold metadata.
32	 */
33	export type EngineStatus = 'pending' | 'running' | 'completed' | 'failed' | 'cancelled' | 'skipped';
34	
35	// ── Engine attempt history ────────────────────────────────────────────
36	
37	/**
38	 * A single engine attempt — one dispatch of the engine's run(), from
39	 * the moment the dispatcher picks it up through its terminal outcome.
40	 *
41	 * Entries are append-on-start: tryRun pushes a row with `startedAt` when
42	 * dispatching, and the terminal handler (or the success path) patches the
43	 * tail row with `endedAt`, `status`, and `error`/`yields` at completion.
44	 *
45	 * Scalar engine-level `startedAt`/`completedAt`/`error`/`sessionId`/`yields`
46	 * fields do not exist — `attempts[-1]` is authoritative.
47	 */
48	export interface EngineAttempt {
49	  /** ISO timestamp when the attempt started. */
50	  startedAt: string;
51	  /** ISO timestamp when the attempt terminated; absent while in-flight. */
52	  endedAt?: string;
53	  /**
54	   * Terminal attempt status — only the two terminal outcomes a single
55	   * attempt can reach. `'completed'` means the attempt produced yields;
56	   * `'failed'` means the attempt threw or observed a non-rate-limit
57	   * session terminal. Absent while the attempt is still in-flight.
58	   */
59	  status?: 'completed' | 'failed';
60	  /** Error message if the attempt terminated in `'failed'`. */
61	  error?: string;
62	  /** Animator session id associated with this attempt, if any. */
63	  sessionId?: string;
64	  /** Yields produced by this attempt, if `status === 'completed'`. */
65	  yields?: unknown;
66	}
67	
68	// ── Engine instance ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
69	
70	/**
71	 * A single engine slot within a rig.
72	 *
73	 * `id` is the engine's position identifier (e.g. 'draft', 'implement').
74	 * For the static pipeline it matches `designId`.
75	 *
76	 * `givensSpec` holds values set at spawn time (writ, role, commands) and
77	 * may contain unresolved yield expression strings (`${yields.<id>.<path>}`)
78	 * that the Spider resolves at run time from upstream engine yields.
79	 *
80	 * Hold metadata (`holdUntil`, `holdReason`, `holdCondition`, `lastCheckedAt`)
81	 * is present while the engine is in `'pending'` due to a retry back-off
82	 * window or an external-gate BlockType. It is cleared when the hold is
83	 * resolved (by poll-clear, by window expiration, or by operator resume).
84	 *
85	 * `attempts[]` is the append-only per-dispatch history for this engine.
86	 * The latest entry carries the in-flight or most recently completed
87	 * attempt's timestamps, session id, yields, and error.
88	 */
89	export interface EngineInstance {
90	  /** Unique identifier within the rig (e.g. 'draft', 'implement'). */
91	  id: string;
92	  /** The engine design to look up in the Fabricator. */
93	  designId: string;
94	  /** Current execution status. */
95	  status: EngineStatus;
96	  /** Engine IDs that must be completed before this engine can run. */
97	  upstream: string[];
98	  /**
99	   * Givens values. Spawn-time expressions (`${writ}`, `${writ.*}`, `${vars.*}`)
100	   * are resolved to their values. Yield expressions (`${yields.*}`) remain as
101	   * literal `${yields.*}` strings and are resolved at run time when the engine
102	   * is executed.
103	   */
104	  givensSpec: Record<string, unknown>;
105	  /**
106	   * Conditional activation expression, copied from the template.
107	   * Evaluated at runtime when upstream is all done. Absent means unconditional.
108	   */
109	  when?: string;
110	  /**
111	   * Per-dispatch history. Each entry records one attempt's lifecycle.
112	   * Downstream code reads `attempts[attempts.length - 1]` for the latest
113	   * state (sessionId, yields, error). An empty array means the engine
114	   * has never been dispatched.
115	   */
116	  attempts?: EngineAttempt[];
117	  /**
118	   * Retry-budget counter. Incremented only when the failure handler
119	   * routes a terminal attempt to the retryable-within-budget branch. A
120	   * rate-limit hold does not increment; a terminal-failed outcome does
121	   * not increment (it records the final consumed attempt via attempts[]).
122	   */
123	  attemptCount?: number;
124	  /**
125	   * ISO timestamp this engine may not dispatch before. Set by the retry
126	   * back-off path; also set by BlockTypes whose hold carries a natural
127	   * deadline. When undefined, the hold is purely gate-driven (the
128	   * BlockType's `check()` result decides readiness).
129	   */
130	  holdUntil?: string;
131	  /**
132	   * BlockType id describing why the engine is being held. When set to a
133	   * value registered in the block type registry, the dispatch predicate
134	   * delegates to that BlockType's `check()` for external-gate
135	   * evaluation. The internal sentinel `'retry-backoff'` is purely
136	   * timer-driven and is not registered in the registry — the predicate
137	   * relies on `holdUntil` alone to clear it. Well-known registered
138	   * values include `'animator-paused'` (the rate-limit gate — the
139	   * engine-failure path writes this id when a session reports a
140	   * rate-limit terminal), `'writ-phase'`, `'scheduled-time'`,
141	   * `'patron-input'`, and `'book-updated'`.
142	   */
143	  holdReason?: string;
144	  /**
145	   * Structured payload validated by the BlockType's `conditionSchema`.
146	   * Carries the specifics needed by `check()` (e.g. `{ sessionId }` for
147	   * `animator-paused`). Shape is opaque to the Spider.
148	   */
149	  holdCondition?: unknown;
150	  /**
151	   * ISO timestamp of the last dispatch-predicate check on this hold.
152	   * The predicate honours the BlockType's `pollIntervalMs` against this
153	   * stamp — `check()` only re-runs after the interval elapses.
154	   */
155	  lastCheckedAt?: string;
156	}
157	
158	// ── Rig ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
159	
160	/**
161	 * Rig status — the full lifecycle of a rig. Computed as a pure projection
162	 * of the rig's engine states plus the operator-cancel marker; never
163	 * written independently.
164	 *
165	 * 'running'   — at least one engine is non-terminal.
166	 * 'completed' — every engine is terminal and at least one completed.
167	 * 'failed'    — some engine terminally failed and no engine is running
168	 *               (i.e. the rig reached a dead end via engine failure).
169	 * 'cancelled' — operator cancel (signalled by a `cancelledAt` stamp).
170	 *
171	 * Legacy rig docs persisted with `'stuck'` or `'blocked'` predate this
172	 * commission; readers must tolerate those string values without crashing.
173	 */
174	export type RigStatus = 'running' | 'completed' | 'failed' | 'cancelled';
175	
176	/**
177	 * A rig — the execution context for a single writ.
178	 *
179	 * Stored in The Stacks (`spider/rigs` book). The `engines` array is the
180	 * ordered pipeline of engine instances. The Spider updates this document
181	 * in-place as engines run and complete.
182	 */
183	export interface RigDoc {
184	  /** Index signature required to satisfy BookEntry constraint. */
185	  [key: string]: unknown;
186	  /** Unique rig id. */
187	  id: string;
188	  /** The writ this rig is executing. */
189	  writId: string;
190	  /** Current rig status. */
191	  status: RigStatus;
192	  /** Ordered engine pipeline. */
193	  engines: EngineInstance[];
194	  /** ISO timestamp when the rig was created. */
195	  createdAt: string;
196	  /**
197	   * ISO timestamp recorded the first time the rig enters a terminal status
198	   * (`completed`, `failed`, `cancelled`). Keep-first semantics:
199	   * subsequent terminal transitions do NOT overwrite this value — it
200	   * pins the moment the rig first stopped making forward progress.
201	   * Absent on rigs that predate this field; the dashboard falls back to
202	   * the latest `attempts[-1].endedAt` for those.
203	   */
204	  terminalAt?: string;
205	  /**
206	   * ISO timestamp recorded when the rig is cancelled by explicit operator
207	   * action (via `SpiderApi.cancel`). The rig-status rollup short-circuits
208	   * to `'cancelled'` when this is set, distinguishing operator-cancel
209	   * from cascade-by-upstream cancel.
210	   */
211	  cancelledAt?: string;
212	  /** Engine id whose yields provide the resolution summary. Set at spawn time. */
213	  resolutionEngineId?: string;
214	}
215	
216	// ── Rig view (UI-facing read shape) ───────────────────────────────────
217	
218	/**
219	 * Aggregated cost summary for a rig — sum across all engines that have a
220	 * sessionId. Absent when no engines have reported any cost data yet.
221	 *
222	 * Token counts are optional because not every provider/session reports
223	 * token usage. When tokenUsage is absent on every contributing session,
224	 * `inputTokens` / `outputTokens` will be undefined; the UI uses this to
225	 * decide whether to render the `(N input, M output)` parenthetical.
226	 */
227	export interface RigCostSummary {
228	  /** Total cost in USD across all sessions. */
229	  costUsd: number;
230	  /** Sum of input tokens across all sessions that reported tokenUsage. Undefined if no session reported. */
231	  inputTokens?: number;
232	  /** Sum of output tokens across all sessions that reported tokenUsage. Undefined if no session reported. */
233	  outputTokens?: number;
234	}
235	
236	/**
237	 * Per-engine cost snapshot. Same shape as the rig-level `RigCostSummary`.
238	 * Present in the `engineCosts` map for every engine that has a sessionId
239	 * (regardless of engine status) — the value may be all-zero when the
240	 * session has not reported cost yet.
241	 */
242	export interface EngineCostSummary {
243	  /** Cost in USD from the engine's session, or 0 if not reported. */
244	  costUsd: number;
245	  /** Input tokens if reported. */
246	  inputTokens?: number;
247	  /** Output tokens if reported. */
248	  outputTokens?: number;
249	}
250	
251	/**
252	 * UI-facing rig view — the persisted `RigDoc` enriched with derived fields
253	 * that the Spider dashboard needs. Returned from the rig API read path
254	 * (rig-list / rig-show). The persisted `RigDoc` shape is unchanged.
255	 *
256	 * - `costSummary` is the rig-level aggregate (sum across all engines with
257	 *   a sessionId). Omitted when no engine has a sessionId.
258	 * - `engineCosts` maps engineId → per-engine cost snapshot. Contains an
259	 *   entry for every engine with a sessionId. Omitted when no engine has
260	 *   a sessionId.
261	 * - `writTitle` is the current title of this rig's writ, joined from the
262	 *   `clerk/writs` book. Omitted when the writ cannot be resolved (e.g.
263	 *   deleted). Derived-only — never persisted on `RigDoc`; recomputed on
264	 *   every read so `writ-edit` title changes are reflected on the next
265	 *   dashboard poll.
266	 *
267	 * All derived fields are read-only reports — they are never persisted.
268	 */
269	export interface RigView extends RigDoc {
270	  /** Rig-level cost aggregate (sum across all engine sessions). */
271	  costSummary?: RigCostSummary;
272	  /** Per-engine cost snapshot keyed by engine id. */
273	  engineCosts?: Record<string, EngineCostSummary>;
274	  /** Current title of this rig's writ, joined from `clerk/writs`. */
275	  writTitle?: string;
276	}
277	
278	// ── Rig filters ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
279	
280	/**
281	 * Filters for listing rigs.
282	 *
283	 * `status` accepts the current four-value `RigStatus` plus the legacy
284	 * `'stuck'` and `'blocked'` strings so operators can still filter for
285	 * rigs persisted before the engine-level retry reshape. New rigs never
286	 * write the legacy values, but the filter tolerates them.
287	 */
288	export interface RigFilters {
289	  /** Filter by rig status. */
290	  status?: RigStatus | 'stuck' | 'blocked';
291	  /** Maximum number of results (default: 20). */
292	  limit?: number;
293	  /** Number of results to skip. */
294	  offset?: number;
295	}
296	
297	// ── Rig templates ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
298	
299	/**
300	 * A single engine slot declared in a rig template.
301	 */
302	export interface RigTemplateEngine {
303	  /** Engine id unique within this template. */
304	  id: string;
305	  /** Engine design id to look up in the Fabricator. */
306	  designId: string;
307	  /** Engine ids within this template whose completion is required first. Defaults to []. */
308	  upstream?: string[];
309	  /**
310	   * Givens to pass to the engine.
311	   *
312	   * String values may contain `${...}` template expressions:
313	   *   `${writ}` — the full WritDoc for this rig's writ
314	   *   `${writ.<path>}` — a field of the WritDoc (dot-path traversal)
315	   *   `${vars.<path>}` — value from spider.variables config (dot-path traversal)
316	   *   `${yields.<engine_id>.<path>}` — a property from an upstream engine's
317	   *       yields (resolved at run time, dot-path traversal)
318	   *
319	   * When a string is exactly one expression (e.g. `${writ}`), the resolved
320	   * value preserves its original type. When expressions are embedded in a
321	   * larger string, the result is always a string.
322	   *
323	   * Non-string values are passed through literally.
324	   * Whole-value expressions that resolve to undefined cause the key to be omitted.
325	   * Inline expressions that resolve to undefined are replaced with empty string.
326	   *
327	   * Use `\${` to produce a literal `${` in the output without interpolation.
328	   */
329	  givens?: Record<string, unknown>;
330	  /**
331	   * Conditional activation expression. A `${yields.<engine_id>.<property>}` reference
332	   * (with optional `!` negation prefix) evaluated at runtime when the engine's upstream
333	   * is all done. When the condition is falsy, the engine is set to `skipped` status.
334	   * When absent, the engine is unconditional (always runs).
335	   *
336	   * Examples:
337	   *   '${yields.review.passed}'  — run this engine when review.passed is truthy
338	   *   '!${yields.review.passed}' — run this engine when review.passed is falsy
339	   */
340	  when?: string;
341	}
342	
343	/**
344	 * A complete rig template.
345	 */
346	export interface RigTemplate {
347	  /** Ordered list of engine slot declarations. */
348	  engines: RigTemplateEngine[];
349	  /**
350	   * Engine id whose yields provide the writ resolution summary.
351	   * Falls back to seal engine, then last completed engine in array order.
352	   */
353	  resolutionEngine?: string;
354	}
355	
356	// ── Retry policy on engine designs ────────────────────────────────────
357	
358	/**
359	 * Back-off config for an engine's retry policy. Matches the shape of
360	 * `AnimatorRateLimitBackoffConfig`: `initialMs` is the first attempt's
361	 * hold; each subsequent attempt multiplies by `factor`, capped at `maxMs`.
362	 */
363	export interface EngineRetryBackoffConfig {
364	  /** First attempt's hold window in milliseconds. */
365	  initialMs: number;
366	  /** Cap on the hold window in milliseconds. */
367	  maxMs: number;
368	  /** Back-off growth factor. Must be > 1. */
369	  factor: number;
370	}
371	
372	/**
373	 * Opt-in retry policy for an engine design. When absent, the effective
374	 * policy is `maxAttempts: 0` — the engine fails terminally on the first
375	 * transient error with no retry. Explicit config enables retry.
376	 *
377	 * Validated at engine-design registration time (see
378	 * `validateEngineRetryConfig`); malformed values throw at startup.
379	 */
380	export interface EngineRetryConfig {
381	  /**
382	   * Total retry budget. `0` means fail fast (never retry — same as
383	   * absent). `1` means one retry (so up to two attempts total). Matches
384	   * the "attempts consumed from budget" semantics in the commission brief.
385	   */
386	  maxAttempts: number;
387	  /** Back-off growth parameters. Optional; defaults applied when omitted. */
388	  backoff?: Partial<EngineRetryBackoffConfig>;
389	}
390	
391	// ── CrawlResult ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
392	
393	/**
394	 * The result of a single crawl() call.
395	 *
396	 * Variants, ordered by priority:
397	 * - 'engine-completed'  — an engine finished (collected or ran inline); rig still running
398	 * - 'engine-started'    — launched a quick engine's session
399	 * - 'engine-held'       — engine entered pending+hold status (rate-limit or
400	 *                         block type gate); rig is still running (other
401	 *                         engines may be active)
402	 * - 'engine-retrying'   — an engine observed a retryable terminal failure
403	 *                         and is pending a back-off window before the
404	 *                         next attempt dispatches
405	 * - 'engine-skipped'    — engine's `when` evaluated false; optional
406	 *                         cascadeSkipped list when downstream conditionals
407	 *                         cascade-skipped
408	 * - 'engine-grafted'    — a graft was applied; downstream engines added
409	 * - 'rig-spawned'       — created a new rig for a ready writ
410	 * - 'rig-completed'     — the crawl step caused a rig to reach a terminal state
411	 * - 'writ-unstuck'      — a writ that Spider previously stuck via the gating
412	 *                         path returned to `open` because its recorded causes
413	 *                         resolved (all failed blockers reached success, or
414	 *                         a cycle was broken by external action).
415	 *
416	 * null means no work was available this tick. This includes the case where
417	 * every candidate open writ was gated on non-terminal follows-blockers —
418	 * the crawl loop skips gated candidates internally and returns null if
419	 * no dispatchable writ was found. Gate state lives on the writ substrate
420	 * (phase + status.spider), not in the CrawlResult.
421	 */
422	export type CrawlResult =
423	  | { action: 'engine-completed'; rigId: string; engineId: string }
424	  | { action: 'engine-started'; rigId: string; engineId: string }
425	  | { action: 'engine-held'; rigId: string; engineId: string; holdReason: string }
426	  | { action: 'engine-retrying'; rigId: string; engineId: string; attemptCount: number }
427	  | { action: 'engine-skipped'; rigId: string; engineId: string; cascadeSkipped?: string[] }
428	  | { action: 'engine-grafted'; rigId: string; engineId: string; graftedEngineIds: string[] }
429	  | { action: 'rig-spawned'; rigId: string; writId: string }
430	  | { action: 'rig-completed'; rigId: string; writId: string; outcome: 'completed' | 'failed' | 'cancelled' }
431	  | { action: 'writ-unstuck'; writId: string };
432	
433	// ── Block type ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
434	
435	/**
436	 * Result of a block type check.
437	 *
438	 * 'cleared' — condition met, unblock the engine.
439	 * 'pending' — condition not yet met, keep polling.
440	 * 'failed'  — condition is permanently unresolvable, fail the engine.
441	 *
442	 * When status is 'failed', an optional reason provides a human-readable
443	 * explanation that the Spider includes in the engine error message.
444	 */
445	export interface CheckResult {
446	  status: 'cleared' | 'pending' | 'failed';
447	  reason?: string;
448	}
449	
450	/** Summary info for a registered block type. */
451	export interface BlockTypeInfo {
452	  /** Block type id. */
453	  id: string;
454	  /** Plugin id that contributed this block type. */
455	  pluginId: string;
456	  /** Suggested poll interval in milliseconds, if set. */
457	  pollIntervalMs?: number;
458	}
459	
460	/** Summary info for a registered rig template. */
461	export interface RigTemplateInfo {
462	  /** Template name (plain for config, qualified pluginId.name for kit). */
463	  name: string;
464	  /** 'config' for guild.json templates, or the pluginId for kit-contributed templates. */
465	  source: string;
466	  /** The template definition. */
467	  template: RigTemplate;
468	}
469	
470	/**
471	 * A registered block type — defines how to check whether a blocking
472	 * condition has cleared. Contributed via kit/supportKit `blockTypes`.
473	 */
474	export interface BlockType {
475	  /** Unique identifier (e.g. 'writ-phase', 'scheduled-time'). */
476	  id: string;
477	  /**
478	   * Check whether the blocking condition has been resolved.
479	   *
480	   * Return { status: 'cleared' } when the condition is met.
481	   * Return { status: 'pending' } when the condition is not yet met.
482	   * Return { status: 'failed' } or { status: 'failed', reason: '...' }
483	   * when the condition is permanently unresolvable.
484	   *
485	   * Throwing is reserved for transient errors (network failures, etc.)
486	   * — the engine stays held and the checker is retried next cycle.
487	   */
488	  check: (condition: unknown) => Promise<CheckResult>;
489	  /** Zod schema for validating the condition payload at block time. */
490	  conditionSchema: ZodSchema;
491	  /** Suggested poll interval in milliseconds. If absent, check every crawl cycle. */
492	  pollIntervalMs?: number;
493	}
494	
495	// ── SpiderApi ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
496	
497	/**
498	 * The Spider's public API — retrieved via guild().apparatus<SpiderApi>('spider').
499	 */
500	export interface SpiderApi {
501	  /**
502	   * Execute one step of the crawl loop.
503	   *
504	   * Priority ordering: collect > graft > run > spawn.
505	   * Returns null when no work is available.
506	   */
507	  crawl(): Promise<CrawlResult | null>;
508	
509	  /**
510	   * Show a rig by id. Throws if not found.
511	   */
512	  show(id: string): Promise<RigDoc>;
513	
514	  /**
515	   * List rigs with optional filters, ordered by createdAt descending.
516	   */
517	  list(filters?: RigFilters): Promise<RigDoc[]>;
518	
519	  /**
520	   * Find the rig for a given writ. Returns null if no rig exists.
521	   */
522	  forWrit(writId: string): Promise<RigDoc | null>;
523	
524	  /**
525	   * Clear a hold on a specific pending engine, forcing the dispatch
526	   * predicate to re-evaluate it on the next crawl tick. Throws if the
527	   * engine is not pending or has no hold set.
528	   */
529	  resume(rigId: string, engineId: string): Promise<void>;
530	
531	  /**
532	   * Cancel a running rig. Cancels the active session (if any), marks
533	   * all non-terminal engines as cancelled, rejects pending input
534	   * requests, and transitions the rig to cancelled status.
535	   *
536	   * Idempotent: returns the rig unchanged if it is already in a terminal state.
537	   * Throws if the rig is not found.
538	   */
539	  cancel(rigId: string, options?: { reason?: string }): Promise<RigDoc>;
540	
541	  /**
542	   * Look up a registered block type by ID.
543	   */
544	  getBlockType(id: string): BlockType | undefined;
545	
546	  /**
547	   * List all registered block types with summary info.
548	   */
549	  listBlockTypes(): BlockTypeInfo[];
550	
551	  /**
552	   * List all registered rig templates with provenance info.
553	   */
554	  listTemplates(): RigTemplateInfo[];
555	
556	  /**
557	   * Return the merged effective writ-type → template-name mapping.
558	   * Config mappings override kit mappings for the same writ type.
559	   */
560	  listTemplateMappings(): Record<string, string>;
561	}
562	
563	// ── Configuration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
564	
565	/**
566	 * Spider apparatus configuration — lives under the `spider` key in guild.json.
567	 */
568	export interface SpiderConfig {
569	  /**
570	   * Polling interval for crawlContinual tool (milliseconds).
571	   * Default: 5000.
572	   */
573	  pollIntervalMs?: number;
574	  /**
575	   * Build command to pass to quick engines.
576	   */
577	  buildCommand?: string;
578	  /**
579	   * Test command to pass to quick engines.
580	   */
581	  testCommand?: string;
582	  /**
583	   * Named rig templates. Keys are template names (not writ types).
584	   * Templates are looked up by name via rigTemplateMappings.
585	   * A template named 'default' is used as the fallback when no mapping matches.
586	   */
587	  rigTemplates?: Record<string, RigTemplate>;
588	  /**
589	   * Writ type → rig template name mappings.
590	   * 'default' key is the fallback for unmatched writ types.
591	   * Config mappings override kit-contributed mappings for the same writ type.
592	   */
593	  rigTemplateMappings?: Record<string, string>;
594	  /**
595	   * User-defined variables available in rig template givens via `${vars.<path>}`.
596	   * Values are passed through literally (string, number, boolean, object).
597	   * Variables resolving to undefined (key absent) cause the givens key to be omitted.
598	   */
599	  variables?: Record<string, unknown>;
600	  /**
601	   * Maximum number of engines allowed in a single rig.
602	   * Grafts that would exceed this limit fail the originating engine.
603	   * Default: 50.
604	   */
605	  maxEnginesPerRig?: number;
606	  /**
607	   * Maximum number of engines that may be running concurrently across all rigs.
608	   * Engines beyond this limit stay in `pending` until a slot frees.
609	   * Default: 3.
610	   */
611	  maxConcurrentEngines?: number;
612	  /**
613	   * Maximum number of engines that may be running concurrently within a single rig.
614	   * Engines beyond this limit stay in `pending` until a slot frees.
615	   * Default: 1.
616	   */
617	  maxConcurrentEnginesPerRig?: number;
618	  /**
619	   * Per-design overrides for engine retry policy. Keyed by `EngineDesign.id`.
620	   *
621	   * The override layers on top of the design's declared `retry`, which in
622	   * turn layers on top of the kit's built-in defaults
623	   * (`DEFAULT_ENGINE_RETRY_BACKOFF`). Effective resolution order is:
624	   *
625	   *     override > design.retry > built-in defaults
626	   *
627	   * Operators name only the fields they want to change. An override may
628	   * change `maxAttempts`, any subset of `backoff` fields, or both. An
629	   * override on a design that declares no `retry` is permitted — the
630	   * absent design retry is treated as `{ maxAttempts: 0, backoff: DEFAULT }`,
631	   * so the override's `maxAttempts` enables retry on a previously
632	   * fail-fast design.
633	   *
634	   * Validated fail-loud at Spider startup: an unknown `designId` (one that
635	   * does not appear in `fabricator.listEngineDesigns()`) or any malformed
636	   * field (`maxAttempts < 0`, `maxMs < initialMs`, `factor <= 1`, etc.)
637	   * throws before any engines are scheduled. The override map is re-read
638	   * live from guild config on each retry decision, so guild.json edits
639	   * take effect on the next retry without restarting the daemon.
640	   */
641	  engineRetryOverrides?: Record<string, Partial<EngineRetryConfig>>;
642	}
643	
644	// ── Engine yield shapes ───────────────────────────────────────────────
645	
646	/**
647	 * Yields from the `draft` clockwork engine.
648	 * The Spider stores these in the engine instance and passes them
649	 * to downstream engines via context.upstream['draft'].
650	 */
651	export interface DraftYields {
652	  /** The draft's unique id. */
653	  draftId: string;
654	  /** Codex this draft belongs to. */
655	  codexName: string;
656	  /** Git branch name for the draft. */
657	  branch: string;
658	  /** Absolute filesystem path to the draft's worktree. */
659	  path: string;
660	  /** HEAD commit SHA at the time the draft was opened. Used by review engine to compute diffs. */
661	  baseSha: string;
662	}
663	
664	/**
665	 * Yields from the `seal` clockwork engine.
666	 */
667	export interface SealYields {
668	  /** The commit SHA at head of the target branch after sealing. */
669	  sealedCommit: string;
670	  /** Git strategy used. */
671	  strategy: 'fast-forward' | 'rebase';
672	  /** Number of retry attempts. */
673	  retries: number;
674	  /** Number of inscriptions (commits) sealed. */
675	  inscriptionsSealed: number;
676	}
677	
678	/**
679	 * Yields from the `seal` clockwork engine when it catches a rebase-conflict
680	 * failure from Scriptorium and grafts a manual-merge recovery tail instead
681	 * of failing the rig. The original rig still has a terminal `seal` engine
682	 * holding these yields; the retry seal that runs after the grafted
683	 * manual-merge anima produces standard SealYields under a new engine id.
684	 */
685	export interface SealRecoveryYields {
686	  /** Always false — the initial seal attempt did not succeed. */
687	  ok: false;
688	  /** The Scriptorium error message that triggered recovery. */
689	  reason: string;
690	  /** Always true — flags this record as a recovery handoff. */
691	  grafted: true;
692	}
693	
694	/**
695	 * Yields from the `manual-merge` quick engine when the mender anima
696	 * reconciles the draft branch against the target. A `SUCCESS` marker
697	 * produces these yields; any other outcome throws in `collect()` and
698	 * fails the engine.
699	 */
700	export interface ManualMergeYields {
701	  /** The Animator session id. */
702	  sessionId: string;
703	  /** Always true — the mender emitted `### Merge: SUCCESS`. */
704	  merged: true;
705	}
706	
707	/**
708	 * Yields from the `implement` quick engine.
709	 * Set by the Spider's collect step when the Animator session completes.
710	 */
711	export interface ImplementYields {
712	  /** The Animator session id. */
713	  sessionId: string;
714	  /** Terminal status of the session. */
715	  sessionStatus: 'completed' | 'failed';
716	}
717	
718	/**
719	 * A single mechanical check (build or test) run by the review engine
720	 * before launching the reviewer session.
721	 */
722	export interface MechanicalCheck {
723	  /** Check name. */
724	  name: 'build' | 'test';
725	  /** Whether the command exited with code 0. */
726	  passed: boolean;
727	  /** Combined stdout+stderr, truncated to 4KB. */
728	  output: string;
729	  /** Wall-clock duration of the check in milliseconds. */
730	  durationMs: number;
731	}
732	
733	/**
734	 * Yields from the `review` quick engine.
735	 * Assembled by the Spider's collect step from session.output and session.metadata.
736	 */
737	export interface ReviewYields {
738	  /** The Animator session id. */
739	  sessionId: string;
740	  /** Reviewer's overall assessment — true if the review passed. */
741	  passed: boolean;
742	  /** Structured markdown findings from the reviewer's final message. */
743	  findings: string;
744	  /** Mechanical check results run before the reviewer session. */
745	  mechanicalChecks: MechanicalCheck[];
746	}
747	
748	/**
749	 * Yields from the `revise` quick engine.
750	 * Set by the Spider's collect step when the Animator session completes.
751	 */
752	export interface ReviseYields {
753	  /** The Animator session id. */
754	  sessionId: string;
755	  /** Terminal status of the session. */
756	  sessionStatus: 'completed' | 'failed';
757	}

759	// ── Input request types ──────────────────────────────────────────────
760	
761	export type InputRequestStatus = 'pending' | 'completed' | 'rejected';
762	
763	export interface ChoiceQuestionSpec {
764	  type: 'choice';
765	  /** Human-readable question text. */
766	  label: string;
767	  /** Optional long-form context, explanation, or instructions for this question. */
768	  details?: string;
769	  /** Optional classification tags for filtering and grouping in the UI. */
770	  tags?: string[];
771	  /** Key → display label options map. */
772	  options: Record<string, string>;
773	  /** When true, the patron can supply a freeform answer instead of selecting. */
774	  allowCustom: boolean;
775	}
776	
777	export interface BooleanQuestionSpec {
778	  type: 'boolean';
779	  /** Human-readable question text. */
780	  label: string;
781	  /** Optional long-form context, explanation, or instructions for this question. */
782	  details?: string;
783	  /** Optional classification tags for filtering and grouping in the UI. */
784	  tags?: string[];
785	}
786	
787	export interface TextQuestionSpec {
788	  type: 'text';
789	  /** Human-readable question text. */
790	  label: string;
791	  /** Optional long-form context, explanation, or instructions for this question. */
792	  details?: string;
793	  /** Optional classification tags for filtering and grouping in the UI. */
794	  tags?: string[];
795	}
796	
797	export type QuestionSpec = ChoiceQuestionSpec | BooleanQuestionSpec | TextQuestionSpec;
798	
799	/** Discriminated choice answer — selected from options or freeform custom. */
800	export type ChoiceAnswer = { selected: string } | { custom: string };
801	
802	/**
803	 * Answer value union. Runtime type is determined by the corresponding QuestionSpec:
804	 * - choice → ChoiceAnswer (object with 'selected' or 'custom' key)
805	 * - boolean → boolean
806	 * - text → string
807	 */
808	export type AnswerValue = ChoiceAnswer | boolean | string;
809	
810	/**
811	 * An input request document stored in the spider/input-requests book.
812	 * Created by engines before blocking; answered by patrons via CLI tools.
813	 */
814	export interface InputRequestDoc {
815	  [key: string]: unknown;
816	  /** Unique ID via generateId('ir', 4). */
817	  id: string;
818	  /** Rig this request belongs to. */
819	  rigId: string;
820	  /** Engine that created this request. */
821	  engineId: string;
822	  /** Request lifecycle status. */
823	  status: InputRequestStatus;
824	  /** Optional human-readable context from the engine. */
825	  message?: string;
826	  /** Question key → question spec. */
827	  questions: Record<string, QuestionSpec>;
828	  /** Question key → answer value. Partially filled until completion. */
829	  answers: Record<string, AnswerValue>;
830	  /** Set when status transitions to 'rejected'. */
831	  rejectionReason?: string;
832	  /** ISO timestamp when the request was created. */
833	  createdAt: string;
834	  /** ISO timestamp of the last mutation. */
835	  updatedAt: string;
836	}
837	
838	// ── Spider-extended engine run / collect result types ─────────────────
839	
840	/**
841	 * Spider-extended engine run result. Adds an optional `graft` field
842	 * to the `completed` variant, allowing engines to dynamically append
843	 * new engines to the rig alongside their yields.
844	 *
845	 * Engines that want to graft import this type from @shardworks/spider-apparatus.
846	 * Engines that don't graft use the base EngineRunResult from @shardworks/fabricator-apparatus.
847	 *
848	 * The Spider internally checks for the `graft` property on any completed result
849	 * (duck-typing — the Fabricator type is not modified).
850	 */
851	export type SpiderEngineRunResult =
852	  | { status: 'completed'; yields: unknown; graft?: RigTemplateEngine[]; graftTail?: string }
853	  | { status: 'launched'; sessionId: string }
854	  | { status: 'blocked'; blockType: string; condition: unknown; message?: string };
855	
856	/**
857	 * Spider-extended collect result. When a quick engine's collect() method
858	 * returns an object with a `graft` property (an array), the Spider extracts
859	 * it as a graft request and uses the `yields` property as the engine's yields.
860	 *
861	 * When collect() returns a value without a `graft` array property, the entire
862	 * return value is treated as yields (backward compatible).
863	 */
864	export interface SpiderCollectResult {
865	  yields: unknown;
866	  graft?: RigTemplateEngine[];
867	  graftTail?: string;
868	}
869	
870	// ── status.spider sub-slot shape ────────────────────────────────────────
871	
872	/**
873	 * The reason a writ is stuck as recorded in its `status.spider` sub-slot.
874	 *
875	 * - 'failed-blocker' — at least one outbound `depends-on` blocker
876	 *                      reached `failed`; the dependent was cascaded to
877	 *                      `stuck` directly (not transitively).
878	 * - 'cycle'          — a back-edge was discovered in the `depends-on`
879	 *                      graph during gate evaluation; every cycle member is
880	 *                      stuck with this cause.
881	 *
882	 * The historical `'engine-failure'` value is no longer written by this
883	 * Spider: the engine-failure path now retries in-place within the rig
884	 * (up to the engine design's `maxAttempts` budget) and, on exhaustion,
885	 * transitions the writ directly to `phase='failed'` without writing
886	 * `status.spider.stuckCause`. Readers must tolerate the absent slot.
887	 */
888	export type SpiderStuckCause = 'failed-blocker' | 'cycle';
889	
890	/**
891	 * Shape of the plugin-owned `status.spider` sub-slot as written by the
892	 * Spider's dependency-gating paths.
893	 *
894	 * The slot is absent (not the empty object) on writs Spider has never
895	 * touched. For dependency-recovery stucks (`failed-blocker` / `cycle`),
896	 * the slot carries `stuckCause`, `blockerIds`, and `observedAt`.
897	 *
898	 * The slot is written only on stuck transitions via the dependency
899	 * gating paths and cleared on auto-unstick. Nothing is written while a
900	 * writ is gated-but-not-stuck or during engine-failure retry — the
901	 * engine-failure path transitions rigs straight to `failed` and writs
902	 * straight to `phase='failed'` without writing this slot.
903	 */
904	export interface SpiderWritStatus {
905	  /** Present only while the writ is stuck for a reason Spider recorded. */
906	  stuckCause?: SpiderStuckCause;
907	  /**
908	   * For gating-path stucks, the blockers responsible for the transition.
909	   * For `failed-blocker`, these are the outbound `depends-on` targets
910	   * that reached `failed`. For `cycle`, these are the members of the
911	   * detected cycle (typically including the dependent itself).
912	   */
913	  blockerIds?: string[];
914	  /** ISO timestamp recorded at the moment the stuck transition was taken. */
915	  observedAt?: string;
916	}
917	
918	// Augment GuildConfig so `guild().guildConfig().spider` is typed.
919	declare module '@shardworks/nexus-core' {
920	  interface GuildConfig {
921	    spider?: SpiderConfig;
922	  }
923	}
