content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Cartograph — public types.
3	 *
4	 * The Cartograph contributes three writ types to the Clerk —
5	 * `vision`, `charge`, and `piece` — and shadows each one with a
6	 * typed companion document stored in its own book.
7	 *
8	 * Companion-doc convention follows astrolabe's `PlanDoc`: the doc id
9	 * matches the writ id one-for-one; both rows are written under one
10	 * `stacks.transaction(...)` boundary by `createX`. Vision text lives on
11	 * `writ.body`; the companion doc carries typed metadata only — the
12	 * minimal field set is deliberate (D14 in the commission spec).
13	 *
14	 * Stage enums are per-type so each carries the domain-level vocabulary
15	 * relevant to its level of the decomposition ladder (see D10 in the
16	 * commission spec).
17	 */
18	
19	import type { WritPhase } from '@shardworks/clerk-apparatus';
20	
21	// ── Stage enums (per type) ───────────────────────────────────────────
22	
23	/**
24	 * Lifecycle stage on a `VisionDoc`. Captures the patron-facing meaning
25	 * of a vision's progression separately from the writ's `phase`:
26	 *
27	 *  - `draft`     — the vision is being shaped; not yet active.
28	 *  - `active`    — the vision is in flight.
29	 *  - `sunset`    — the patron retired the vision (graceful wind-down).
30	 *  - `cancelled` — the vision was cancelled before reaching `sunset`.
31	 *
32	 * The typed-API `transitionVision` helper is the only sanctioned path
33	 * that updates this field; it writes both `writ.phase` and `stage`
34	 * inside one transaction so the two never drift.
35	 */
36	export type VisionStage = 'draft' | 'active' | 'sunset' | 'cancelled';
37	
38	/**
39	 * Lifecycle stage on a `ChargeDoc`. The `validated` value records the
40	 * patron-walkthrough acceptance moment that distinguishes a charge from
41	 * a piece:
42	 *
43	 *  - `draft`      — the charge is being shaped.
44	 *  - `active`     — the charge is in flight under its parent vision.
45	 *  - `validated`  — the patron walked through and accepted the charge.
46	 *  - `dropped`    — the charge was abandoned without acceptance.
47	 */
48	export type ChargeStage = 'draft' | 'active' | 'validated' | 'dropped';
49	
50	/**
51	 * Lifecycle stage on a `PieceDoc`. Pieces are internal organization;
52	 * the `done` value records completion as a structural fact rather than
53	 * a patron-walkthrough fact:
54	 *
55	 *  - `draft`   — the piece is being shaped.
56	 *  - `active`  — the piece is in flight.
57	 *  - `done`    — the piece is complete.
58	 *  - `dropped` — the piece was abandoned.
59	 */
60	export type PieceStage = 'draft' | 'active' | 'done' | 'dropped';
61	
62	// ── Companion documents ──────────────────────────────────────────────
63	
64	/**
65	 * Companion document for a `vision` writ. Keyed by the writ id one-for-
66	 * one. Carries typed metadata only — vision text lives on `writ.body`.
67	 *
68	 * The `[key: string]: unknown` index signature satisfies Stacks'
69	 * `BookEntry` constraint and lets future commissions grow the field set
70	 * non-breakingly. The patch surface mirrors astrolabe's `PlanDoc.patch`.
71	 */
72	export interface VisionDoc {
73	  /** Index signature required to satisfy BookEntry. */
74	  [key: string]: unknown;
75	  /** The vision writ's id — primary key, matches the writ id. */
76	  id: string;
77	  /** Lifecycle stage. Coupled to `writ.phase` by the typed-API transition helpers. */
78	  stage: VisionStage;
79	  /** Codex this vision targets. Inherited from the writ at creation time. */
80	  codex?: string;
81	  /** ISO timestamp when the doc was created. */
82	  createdAt: string;
83	  /** ISO timestamp of the last mutation. */
84	  updatedAt: string;
85	}
86	
87	/**
88	 * Companion document for a `charge` writ. Same shape rules as
89	 * `VisionDoc` — keyed by writ id, minimal fields, index signature for
90	 * forward compatibility.
91	 */
92	export interface ChargeDoc {
93	  /** Index signature required to satisfy BookEntry. */
94	  [key: string]: unknown;
95	  /** The charge writ's id — primary key, matches the writ id. */
96	  id: string;
97	  /** Lifecycle stage. Coupled to `writ.phase` by the typed-API transition helpers. */
98	  stage: ChargeStage;
99	  /** Codex this charge targets. Inherited from the writ at creation time. */
100	  codex?: string;
101	  /** ISO timestamp when the doc was created. */
102	  createdAt: string;
103	  /** ISO timestamp of the last mutation. */
104	  updatedAt: string;
105	}
106	
107	/**
108	 * Companion document for a `piece` writ. Same shape rules as
109	 * `VisionDoc`/`ChargeDoc` — keyed by writ id, minimal fields, index
110	 * signature for forward compatibility.
111	 */
112	export interface PieceDoc {
113	  /** Index signature required to satisfy BookEntry. */
114	  [key: string]: unknown;
115	  /** The piece writ's id — primary key, matches the writ id. */
116	  id: string;
117	  /** Lifecycle stage. Coupled to `writ.phase` by the typed-API transition helpers. */
118	  stage: PieceStage;
119	  /** Codex this piece targets. Inherited from the writ at creation time. */
120	  codex?: string;
121	  /** ISO timestamp when the doc was created. */
122	  createdAt: string;
123	  /** ISO timestamp of the last mutation. */
124	  updatedAt: string;
125	}
126	
127	// ── Filters ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
128	
129	/**
130	 * Filters for listing visions. Mirrors astrolabe's `PlanFilters` shape
131	 * with `stage` replacing `status`. Lists are ordered by `createdAt desc`.
132	 */
133	export interface VisionFilters {
134	  /** Filter by stage. */
135	  stage?: VisionStage;
136	  /** Filter by codex. */
137	  codex?: string;
138	  /** Maximum number of results (default: 20). */
139	  limit?: number;
140	  /** Number of results to skip. */
141	  offset?: number;
142	}
143	
144	/** Filters for listing charges. Mirrors `VisionFilters`. */
145	export interface ChargeFilters {
146	  /** Filter by stage. */
147	  stage?: ChargeStage;
148	  /** Filter by codex. */
149	  codex?: string;
150	  /** Maximum number of results (default: 20). */
151	  limit?: number;
152	  /** Number of results to skip. */
153	  offset?: number;
154	}
155	
156	/** Filters for listing pieces. Mirrors `VisionFilters`. */
157	export interface PieceFilters {
158	  /** Filter by stage. */
159	  stage?: PieceStage;
160	  /** Filter by codex. */
161	  codex?: string;
162	  /** Maximum number of results (default: 20). */
163	  limit?: number;
164	  /** Number of results to skip. */
165	  offset?: number;
166	}
167	
168	// ── Create requests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
169	
170	/**
171	 * Request to create a top-level vision. Visions have no parent — passing
172	 * a non-empty `parentId` is rejected by the typed API.
173	 */
174	export interface CreateVisionRequest {
175	  /** Short human-readable title describing the vision. */
176	  title: string;
177	  /** Long-form vision text, stored on `writ.body`. */
178	  body: string;
179	  /** Optional target codex. */
180	  codex?: string;
181	}
182	
183	/**
184	 * Request to create a charge under a vision. The typed API rejects when
185	 * `parentId` is missing or when the parent's writ type is not `vision`.
186	 */
187	export interface CreateChargeRequest {
188	  /** Required: must reference an existing vision in a non-terminal state. */
189	  parentId: string;
190	  /** Short human-readable title describing the charge. */
191	  title: string;
192	  /** Detail text, stored on `writ.body`. */
193	  body: string;
194	  /** Optional target codex. Defaults to the parent vision's codex. */
195	  codex?: string;
196	}
197	
198	/**
199	 * Request to create a piece under a charge or piece. The typed API
200	 * rejects when `parentId` is missing or when the parent's writ type is
201	 * not `charge` or `piece`.
202	 */
203	export interface CreatePieceRequest {
204	  /** Required: must reference an existing charge or piece in a non-terminal state. */
205	  parentId: string;
206	  /** Short human-readable title describing the piece. */
207	  title: string;
208	  /** Detail text, stored on `writ.body`. */
209	  body: string;
210	  /** Optional target codex. Defaults to the parent's codex. */
211	  codex?: string;
212	}
213	
214	// ── Transition requests ──────────────────────────────────────────────
215	
216	/**
217	 * Lifecycle-coupled transition request for a vision. The caller supplies
218	 * both the target `phase` and the target `stage` because the mapping is
219	 * not always one-to-one — for example, a vision driven to `failed` might
220	 * mean stage `cancelled` or stage `sunset` depending on context. The
221	 * typed API writes both fields atomically.
222	 */
223	export interface TransitionVisionRequest {
224	  /** Target phase on the underlying writ. */
225	  phase: WritPhase;
226	  /** Target stage on the companion VisionDoc. */
227	  stage: VisionStage;
228	  /** Optional resolution string. Set on terminal transitions. */
229	  resolution?: string;
230	}
231	
232	/**
233	 * Lifecycle-coupled transition request for a charge. See
234	 * `TransitionVisionRequest` for why both `phase` and `stage` are
235	 * specified explicitly.
236	 */
237	export interface TransitionChargeRequest {
238	  /** Target phase on the underlying writ. */
239	  phase: WritPhase;
240	  /** Target stage on the companion ChargeDoc. */
241	  stage: ChargeStage;
242	  /** Optional resolution string. Set on terminal transitions. */
243	  resolution?: string;
244	}
245	
246	/**
247	 * Lifecycle-coupled transition request for a piece. See
248	 * `TransitionVisionRequest` for why both `phase` and `stage` are
249	 * specified explicitly.
250	 */
251	export interface TransitionPieceRequest {
252	  /** Target phase on the underlying writ. */
253	  phase: WritPhase;
254	  /** Target stage on the companion PieceDoc. */
255	  stage: PieceStage;
256	  /** Optional resolution string. Set on terminal transitions. */
257	  resolution?: string;
258	}
259	
260	// ── API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
261	
262	/**
263	 * The `provides` interface for the Cartograph apparatus.
264	 *
265	 * The typed-API surface is the **only** layer that enforces the ladder's
266	 * parent invariants:
267	 *
268	 *   - `createVision` rejects any non-empty `parentId`.
269	 *   - `createCharge` rejects unless the parent's writ type is `vision`.
270	 *   - `createPiece`  rejects unless the parent's writ type is `charge` or `piece`.
271	 *
272	 * Raw `clerk.post({ type: 'vision' })` continues to succeed without
273	 * parent-type checks — the typed API is the validator. WritTypeConfig
274	 * deliberately carries no parentTypes/allowedChildren restrictions.
275	 *
276	 * Each `createX` opens a single Stacks transaction and writes both the
277	 * writ row and the companion document inside one boundary. Parent
278	 * existence, parent-not-terminal, codex inheritance, and id generation
279	 * mirror Clerk's `post()` validation byte-for-byte (the duplication is
280	 * the cost of being a typed atomic surface).
281	 *
282	 * Each `transitionX` writes both `writ.phase` and the companion doc's
283	 * `stage` field atomically. The caller specifies both targets
284	 * explicitly because a single phase may map to multiple stages
285	 * depending on context.
286	 */
287	export interface CartographApi {
288	  // ── Vision ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
289	
290	  /** Create a top-level vision. Rejects when `parentId` is set. */
291	  createVision(request: CreateVisionRequest): Promise<VisionDoc>;
292	  /** Show a vision by writ id. Throws if the doc is not found. */
293	  showVision(id: string): Promise<VisionDoc>;
294	  /** List visions, ordered by createdAt descending. */
295	  listVisions(filters?: VisionFilters): Promise<VisionDoc[]>;
296	  /** Patch a vision's companion doc. Returns the updated document. */
297	  patchVision(id: string, fields: Partial<Omit<VisionDoc, 'id'>>): Promise<VisionDoc>;
298	  /** Atomically transition both the writ phase and the companion stage. */
299	  transitionVision(id: string, request: TransitionVisionRequest): Promise<VisionDoc>;
300	
301	  // ── Charge ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
302	
303	  /** Create a charge under a vision. Rejects when the parent is not a vision. */
304	  createCharge(request: CreateChargeRequest): Promise<ChargeDoc>;
305	  /** Show a charge by writ id. Throws if the doc is not found. */
306	  showCharge(id: string): Promise<ChargeDoc>;
307	  /** List charges, ordered by createdAt descending. */
308	  listCharges(filters?: ChargeFilters): Promise<ChargeDoc[]>;
309	  /** Patch a charge's companion doc. Returns the updated document. */
310	  patchCharge(id: string, fields: Partial<Omit<ChargeDoc, 'id'>>): Promise<ChargeDoc>;
311	  /** Atomically transition both the writ phase and the companion stage. */
312	  transitionCharge(id: string, request: TransitionChargeRequest): Promise<ChargeDoc>;
313	
314	  // ── Piece ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
315	
316	  /** Create a piece under a charge or piece. Rejects on any other parent type. */
317	  createPiece(request: CreatePieceRequest): Promise<PieceDoc>;
318	  /** Show a piece by writ id. Throws if the doc is not found. */
319	  showPiece(id: string): Promise<PieceDoc>;
320	  /** List pieces, ordered by createdAt descending. */
321	  listPieces(filters?: PieceFilters): Promise<PieceDoc[]>;
322	  /** Patch a piece's companion doc. Returns the updated document. */
323	  patchPiece(id: string, fields: Partial<Omit<PieceDoc, 'id'>>): Promise<PieceDoc>;
324	  /** Atomically transition both the writ phase and the companion stage. */
325	  transitionPiece(id: string, request: TransitionPieceRequest): Promise<PieceDoc>;
326	}
327

1	/**
2	 * Reckoner public types.
3	 *
4	 * The Reckoner is a narrow MVP observer: a Phase 2 CDC watcher on
5	 * `clerk/writs` that emits Lattice pulses for three trigger types
6	 * (`reckoner.writ-stuck`, `reckoner.writ-failed`, `reckoner.queue-drained`).
7	 * It has no persistent state and no user-facing configuration.
8	 */
9	
10	/**
11	 * Runtime API exposed by the Reckoner.
12	 *
13	 * The Reckoner runs autonomously via CDC; its public API is intentionally
14	 * small — just enough for surfaces that want to list or inspect the trigger
15	 * types the Reckoner produces.
16	 */
17	export interface ReckonerApi {
18	  /** Emitter plugin id — stamped on `pulse.source` for every pulse this observer emits. */
19	  readonly source: string;
20	  /** The three trigger types this observer produces. */
21	  readonly triggerTypes: readonly string[];
22	}
23	
24	// ── Context payloads per trigger type (D30) ──────────────────────
25	
26	/**
27	 * Dedupe-identity field present on every Reckoner context payload.
28	 *
29	 * The triggering writ's `updatedAt` stamp — every Clerk transition bumps
30	 * `updatedAt`, so `(writId, triggerType, writUpdatedAt)` is a true
31	 * per-transition identity. A CDC replay fires with the same `updatedAt`
32	 * and is suppressed by the emitter's idempotency guard; a legitimate
33	 * re-visit of the same phase pair gets a fresh `updatedAt` and a fresh
34	 * pulse. See `docs/architecture/apparatus/sentinel.md` §"Idempotency
35	 * under replay".
36	 *
37	 * For `QueueDrainedContext`, this carries the triggering (terminal)
38	 * writ's `updatedAt` alongside the existing `lastTerminalWritId`.
39	 */
40	
41	/** Context payload for `reckoner.writ-stuck`. */
42	export interface WritStuckContext {
43	  /** Two-segment short id (`w-abc123`). */
44	  writShortId: string;
45	  /** Phase at emit time — always `'stuck'` here. */
46	  writPhase: 'stuck';
47	  /** Current title of the writ. */
48	  writTitle: string;
49	  /** Writ type. */
50	  writType: string;
51	  /** Dedupe-identity: triggering writ's `updatedAt` at emit time. */
52	  writUpdatedAt: string;
53	  /** Stuck cause written by Spider, when available. */
54	  stuckCause?: string;
55	}
56	
57	/**
58	 * Per-attempt summary entry surfaced on `WritFailedContext.engineFailure.attemptsSummary`.
59	 *
60	 * Mirrors a Spider `EngineAttempt` row, less the `yields` payload — the
61	 * pulse is a diagnostic surface, not an audit log, and yields can balloon
62	 * the context size without serving a named consumer.
63	 *
64	 * Every field is optional because the source `EngineAttempt` row keeps
65	 * them optional while an attempt is in flight or terminates without a
66	 * session id.
67	 */
68	export interface EngineAttemptSummary {
69	  /** ISO timestamp when the attempt started. */
70	  startedAt?: string;
71	  /** ISO timestamp when the attempt terminated; absent while in-flight. */
72	  endedAt?: string;
73	  /**
74	   * Terminal attempt status — only the two terminal outcomes a single
75	   * attempt can reach. Absent while the attempt is still in-flight.
76	   */
77	  status?: 'completed' | 'failed';
78	  /** Error message if the attempt terminated in `'failed'`. */
79	  error?: string;
80	  /** Animator session id associated with this attempt, if any. */
81	  sessionId?: string;
82	}
83	
84	/**
85	 * Engine-failure enrichment block on `WritFailedContext`.
86	 *
87	 * Present only when the failed root mandate has a rig in `failed` status
88	 * with at least one engine in `failed` status. Surfaces the design-click
89	 * diagnostic fields so a patron reading the pulse can identify the failed
90	 * engine, attempt count, and per-attempt error trail without dropping
91	 * into `nsg rig show`.
92	 */
93	export interface EngineFailureContext {
94	  /** Rig id whose failed engine produced this enrichment. */
95	  rigId: string;
96	  /** Engine instance id within the rig (e.g. 'draft', 'implement'). */
97	  engineId: string;
98	  /** Engine design id — the Fabricator design key. */
99	  engineDesignId: string;
100	  /**
101	   * Retry budget consumed by the failed engine. Absent on rigs whose
102	   * failed engine never had its retry counter incremented (rare: a
103	   * fail-fast engine with `maxAttempts: 0`).
104	   */
105	  attemptCount?: number;
106	  /**
107	   * Most recent attempt's error message — the `error` field on the tail
108	   * `EngineAttempt` row when its `status === 'failed'`. Absent when the
109	   * tail attempt has no error string.
110	   */
111	  lastError?: string;
112	  /**
113	   * Ordered list of attempt summaries copied from the engine's
114	   * `attempts[]` array. Empty when the engine has never been dispatched
115	   * (which would be unusual on a `failed` engine but is tolerated).
116	   */
117	  attemptsSummary: EngineAttemptSummary[];
118	}
119	
120	/** Context payload for `reckoner.writ-failed`. */
121	export interface WritFailedContext {
122	  /** Two-segment short id. */
123	  writShortId: string;
124	  /** Current title of the writ. */
125	  writTitle: string;
126	  /** Writ type. */
127	  writType: string;
128	  /** Dedupe-identity: triggering writ's `updatedAt` at emit time. */
129	  writUpdatedAt: string;
130	  /** Resolution string recorded on the failed transition. */
131	  resolution?: string;
132	  /** Short ids of failed child writs referenced by the resolution, when parseable. */
133	  childFailures?: string[];
134	  /**
135	   * Optional engine-failure enrichment block. Present when the failed
136	   * mandate has a `failed` rig with a `failed` engine; absent for
137	   * patron-driven failures, cascade-only failures, and rigs whose engines
138	   * are all in non-failed terminal states.
139	   */
140	  engineFailure?: EngineFailureContext;
141	}
142	
143	/** Context payload for `reckoner.queue-drained`. */
144	export interface QueueDrainedContext {
145	  /** ISO timestamp of the drain event. */
146	  drainedAt: string;
147	  /** Id of the writ whose terminal transition brought the queue to drain. */
148	  lastTerminalWritId: string;
149	  /**
150	   * Dedupe-identity: the triggering (terminal) writ's `updatedAt` at
151	   * emit time. Combined with `lastTerminalWritId`, this identifies the
152	   * drain-evaluating transition uniquely across a CDC replay.
153	   */
154	  writUpdatedAt: string;
155	}
156	
157	// ── Trigger type ids ────────────────────────────────────────────
158	
159	export const TRIGGER_WRIT_STUCK = 'reckoner.writ-stuck';
160	export const TRIGGER_WRIT_FAILED = 'reckoner.writ-failed';
161	export const TRIGGER_QUEUE_DRAINED = 'reckoner.queue-drained';
162	
163	/** Plugin id stamped on `pulse.source`. Matches the package's apparatus id. */
164	export const RECKONER_PLUGIN_ID = 'reckoner';
165

content.text:
I've finished the survey. Let me organize the findings before opening anything in the click tree.

---

# Findings: applying `writ.ext` and `writ.status` more broadly

## Background — what the slots are for

- **`status[pluginId]`** — *post-hoc observations* a plugin records about a writ. Spec/status split: phase is the Clerk-owned spec; sub-slots are observational. (e.g. `status['clerk'].triggeringChildId`, `status['spider'].stuckCause`.)
- **`ext[pluginId]`** — *metadata attached at identity time* — petition ids, classifier tags, foreign-system refs. (e.g. `ext['reckoner']` carrying source/priority/payload.)

Both are plugin-keyed maps with one sanctioned write path each; sibling sub-slots survive. They live alongside the writ row, eliminating companion books.

---

## Part 1 — Plugin features that could be cleaned up today

### 🥇 Cartograph: collapse the three companion books

This is the most dramatic candidate by far. **`VisionDoc`, `ChargeDoc`, `PieceDoc`** exist only to attach `{stage, codex, createdAt, updatedAt}` to the writ — pure metadata, no content. The apparatus is 693 lines, and the bulk of it is dual-book transactional ceremony.

Migrate to `writ.ext['cartograph'] = { stage, codex }`:

- Three books deleted: `cartograph/visions`, `cartograph/charges`, `cartograph/pieces`.
- `transitionVision`/`transitionCharge`/`transitionPiece` become trivial — `setWritExt` followed by (or composed inside) the phase transition.
- `createVision`/`createCharge`/`createPiece` collapse to `clerk.post` + `setWritExt`. The transactional pair-write disappears.
- `patchVision`/`patchCharge`/`patchPiece` become a single `setWritExt`.
- Reads (`showVision`, `listVisions`, …) become reads of `clerk.list({ type: 'vision' })` projecting `ext['cartograph']`.

Worth flagging: `createdAt`/`updatedAt` already exist on the writ row, so they don't need to ride in `ext` at all. The companion docs were duplicating writ timestamps.

### 🥈 Animator: split `SessionDoc.metadata` into ext sub-slots

`SessionDoc.metadata` is currently a free-form `Record<string, unknown>` that multiple plugins stamp into uncoordinated:

- Spider stamps `{ engineId, writId }`
- Loom/Animator stamp `{ role, trigger }`
- Parlour stamps conversation/turn ids
- Astrolabe stamps `{ engineId: 'reader-analyst' }`, etc.

The reducer's deep-merge is exactly the workaround you'd write if you didn't have plugin-keyed sub-slots. The `metadata.role` and `metadata.trigger` reads in `session-emission.ts` are already convention-only ownership — `setWritStatus`-pattern without enforcement.

If sessions adopted ext (see Part 2), each producer would get its own sub-slot:

- `ext['spider'] = { engineId, writId }`
- `ext['summon-relay'] = { trigger, role, eventId, eventName }`
- `ext['parlour'] = { conversationId, turnNumber }`

This would also let `terminationDiagnostic` move to `status['animator']` (it's literally a post-hoc observation) and `terminationTag` likewise.

### 🥉 Astrolabe: lift `PlanDoc.status` and `manifestFilesCount` to writ slots

`PlanDoc` is *mostly* content (inventory, scope, decisions, spec, observations) — those should stay in the companion book. But two fields are misplaced:

- `status: PlanStatus` (`reading | analyzing | reviewing | writing | completed | failed`) — that's lifecycle state. Today it lives separately from `writ.phase`, which means readers have to join two books to know "where is this plan." Move it to the writ's phase-machine (register a `plan` writ type with these states) or to `status['astrolabe']` if the Plan is genuinely a different lifecycle from the brief.
- `manifestFilesCount` and `generatedWritId` — pure observation/measurement. `status['astrolabe']` fits.

Less dramatic than Cartograph because the bulk of PlanDoc is content, not metadata, but worth doing for the lifecycle field.

### 🏅 Spider: `SpiderWritStatus` is the prototype — use it more

`status['spider'] = { stuckCause, blockerIds, observedAt }` already exists for dependency-gating stucks. There's room to stamp similar observations in cases that today smuggle structure through the resolution string:

- **Cascade context.** When a writ fails by child-cascade, `status['clerk'].triggeringChildId` is recorded — but the `WritFailedContext` consumer in Sentinel parses short ids out of `resolution` (`childFailures` is parsed text). A `status['clerk'].cascadedChildIds` array would be a structured slot the parser could read directly.
- **Engine-failure terminus.** Sentinel's `EngineFailureContext` re-derives `{rigId, engineId, attemptCount, lastError}` from the rig book on every pulse emit. A small `status['spider'] = { lastFailedEngine: {…} }` snapshot stamped at writ-fail time would let consumers read it without joining `spider/rigs`.

### 🏅 Reckoner already uses ext correctly — but consider migrating part of `ReckoningDoc`

`ext['reckoner']` is the canonical "petition contract" slot. Good. But `ReckoningDoc` is its own book and is *mostly* a journal — projections of decisions and decline/defer reasons. That's correct as a separate book (reckonings are events, not metadata about the writ). However: the `writUpdatedAt` dedupe-identity currently embedded in `ReckoningDoc` could equivalently sit at `status['reckoner'].lastConsideredUpdatedAt` on the writ, making the dedupe check a one-row read instead of a query against the reckonings book.

Probably not worth it — but flagging the option.

---

## Part 2 — Other entities that would benefit from the same structures

### 🥇 Clicks (Ratchet) — strongest fit

`ClickDoc` mirrors `WritDoc` almost perfectly: id, parentId, status (lifecycle), human-readable content (goal/conclusion). Already has typed links. **No** ext/status pattern today — `goalHistory` and `createdSessionId`/`resolvedSessionId` are bare top-level fields.

Concrete pressure points where ext/status would help:

- **Coco's goal-history** — already split into a typed array. If observation-shape, it should live at `status['ratchet'].goalHistory`. Right now any plugin wanting to attach observation data to a click has nowhere to put it.
- **Vocabulary cross-links** — when we open a click and want to remember "this matches a future-vocabulary term," there's no slot. `ext['vocabulary'] = { aliases: [...] }` would be natural.
- **Survey/laboratory observations** — multiple analytical plugins want to tag clicks (e.g. "this click closed in <1s — bookkeeping noise") without polluting the goal/conclusion content. `status['laboratory'] = {...}` is the right home.

**Naming snag**: Click already calls its lifecycle field `status` (= phase). If we adopt the writ pattern verbatim, we'd want to rename ClickStatus → ClickPhase (matching writs) and reserve `status` for the observation slot. That rename is the same shape as the writ rename done earlier.

### 🥈 Sessions (Animator) — solid fit

Already discussed under Part 1's `metadata` migration. SessionDoc is the entity multiple plugins observe (Spider via attempts, Laboratory via CDC, the back-off machine via terminationTag). It has a lifecycle (running → completed/failed/timeout/cancelled/rate-limited). It accumulates observation-shape data (`terminationDiagnostic`, `lastActivityAt`, `output`, `tokenUsage`).

Sub-slot pattern fit: high. Would cleanly separate provider-reported facts (Animator's domain) from observer annotations.

### 🥉 Conversations (Parlour) — moderate fit

`ConversationDoc` has a lifecycle (`active | concluded | abandoned`), participants, and `eventId`. Multiple plugins might want to observe a convene (the Laboratory wants to record outcome quality; future analytics might want to tag conversations). Would benefit from `status['parlour']` and possibly `ext['parlour']` for kind-specific metadata.

Less urgent than clicks/sessions — the Parlour ecosystem has fewer observers today.

### 🏅 Rigs (Spider) — interesting case, weaker fit

`RigDoc` is owned start-to-finish by Spider — there are few external observers writing to it. The pattern wouldn't gain much. But: `engineCosts`/`writTitle` on `RigView` are derived-only and recomputed every read; that's a viewer concern, not an ext concern. Probably leave rigs alone.

### 🏅 Pulses (Lattice) — already doing the right thing differently

`PulseDoc.context: Record<string, unknown>` is keyed by triggerType, not pluginId. Each pulse has exactly one source and one triggerType, so the plugin-keyed shape would always have a single key. The current shape is appropriate; not a candidate.

---

## Recommendations

1. **Take the Cartograph cleanup soon.** This is a high-leverage simplification — three books and ~600 lines collapse to a thin ext-projection. It's also the cleanest demonstration that "companion-doc-as-metadata-store" is a pre-ext pattern that ext supersedes.
2. **Adopt ext sub-slots for SessionDoc.metadata next.** Smaller blast radius, multiple stamping callers, eliminates the deep-merge reducer special case.
3. **Land the click ext/status pattern as a separate piece** — coupled with the ClickStatus→ClickPhase rename. It's mostly forward-looking; current callers don't urgently need it, but every future click-observer plugin will.
4. **Don't migrate Astrolabe's PlanDoc wholesale** — only lift the lifecycle status field. The content payload belongs in its own book.

Want me to open clicks for any of these, or pull one of them into a brief?

content: Surveyed the plugins for places where the new writ ext/status slots could clean up existing patterns, and identified entities that would benefit from the same shape. Next: pick which finding to act on first — Cartograph collapse, SessionDoc metadata split, or click ext/status. (disable recaps in /config)
